name: Haarlem (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: from the city's coat of arms
the silver field charged with the sword and stars

description: A horizontal tricolour of red, white and red, the central white stripe charged with the city's sword and four stars.
history: Haarlem's flag derives from the city's medieval coat of arms; the silver sword charged on a red field, with stars added later, has been the city's emblem since the 13th century.

features.symbolism: the historic civic colours of Haarlem

features.role: Sword from the Haarlem coat of arms
features.symbolism: the silver sword has been the city's emblem since the medieval period
